Sport|Olympics

TheOlympicscontinue on Saturday with another slew of medals set to be won in Paris amid a typically hectic Games schedule.

The women’s 100m final takes centre stage as the athletics action ramps up at the Stade de France, but that is far from the only highlight.

Team GBwill be out to increase their medal tally even further, having set a new national record for the most golds secured in the first week of anOlympic Gameswith nine after a memorable treble on Fantastic Friday.

Great Britain go into Day 8 in Paris tied on golds with Olympic powerhouses the USA,nestled nicely in fifth in the medal table.

All times BST unless stated

Men’s cycling road race - 10am

We’re back out on the road for the first of this weekend’s cycling races spanning 273km on a city circuit featuring plenty of gruelling climbs that both starts and finishes at the Place du Trocadero.

The likes of Mathieu van der Poel, Remco Evenepoel, Wout van Aert and Mads Pedersen will hope to battle for gold.

Mountain bike gold medalist Tom Pidcock, Josh Tarling, Stevie Williams and Fred Wright make up the British contingent.

Men’s pommel horse final - 4:16pm

Max Whitlock, the most successful British gymnast of all-time, will bow out of the sport following Saturday evening’s pommel horse final - but will he do so on top of the world again?

Whitlock, who won gold in this event in both Rio de Janeiro and Tokyo, is looking to become the first man to win three pommel horse titles on the bounce, as well as the first gymnast to win a medal on the same apparatus at four successive Games.

The 31-year-old qualified third for the final but then upped the difficulty of his routine during Monday night’s team final. A third straight fourth-placed finish there has added further fuel.

Women’s 100m final - 8:20pm

All eyes will be on the fast Stade de France purple track on Saturday night for the first of this weekend’s huge 100m finals.

Kate and William try their hand at curling in Stirling

Aquatics GB 'immensely disappointed' as Proud joins Enhanced Games

Sports quiz of 2024: 100 hard questions on an incredible year

Discover a hidden island paradise in the Indian Ocean

We won’t know the exact lineup until after the semi-finals on Saturday, but reigning world champion Sha'Carri Richardson of the USA should be a clear favourite barring disaster after her dominant performance in the heats.

Jamaican rival Shericka Jackson has withdrawn from the 100m event in Paris, but is still running in the 200m.

Compatriot Shelly-Ann Fraser Pryce and Julien Alfred of Saint Lucia will be expected to take the fight to Richardson instead, while Team GB will hope that Dina Asher-Smith and Daryll Neita make it through.

08.30:Rowing- women’s single sculls finals

21.36:Surfing- Women’s bronze medal match

22.12:Surfing- Women’s gold medal match

TV channel:The BBC is broadcasting two live streams of the action for the duration of the Games. This will be on BBC One, BBC Two or a stream available by clicking the red button on Freeview or a smart TV.

On Saturday, it’s 8am BST until 1pm on BBC One and again from 1:15pm until 6pm, plus 6:20pm until 10pm. BBC Two briefly pick up the coverage between 6pm and 6:20pm.

There is reduced coverage compared with previous years, when the BBC broadcasted everything from archery to wrestling live on its website or channels. Instead, Discovery+ is the main rights holder forParis 2024and is showing every moment across the Eurosport channels in the UK from 7am to 10.30pm every day.

Live stream:Fans can also catch the BBC coverage live online for free via the BBC Sport website and BBC iPlayer. The Discovery+ app will be showing extensive coverage, which begins at a monthly fee of £3.99.

Highlights:The BBC will be showing a nightly highlights show called Tonight at the Games, which on Saturday night is being shown on BBC One at 10.20pm.

Live blog:You can follow all the action on Day 8 at the Olympics viaStandard Sport’srolling live blog, with expert analysis from reporters Matt Majendie and Malik Ouzia out in Paris.

title: Today's Olympics schedule: Team GB events, Day 8 medal highlights and how to watch Paris Games | The Standard
author: George Flood
description: A whole host of medals are up for grabs on Saturday, including in the women’s 100m
keywords: Paris 2024, Olympic Games, Team GB, Max Whitlock
